description: Songbird LLM eval set — 75 natural language prompts representing real composer/producer requests

prompts.prompt: Write a 4-bar major pentatonic melody on a lead synth, starting on C4.
prompts.notes: Should produce a p/n pattern with C pentatonic notes (C D E G A)

prompts.prompt: Add a counter-melody that harmonizes a third above the main lead line.
prompts.notes: Adds a new channel with notes offset by +4 semitones (major 3rd)

prompts.prompt: Create a descending chromatic run from G5 to C5 in 16th notes.
prompts.notes: Pattern of 16th notes (x) descending from G5 (79) to C5 (72)

prompts.prompt: Compose an 8-bar melody in the key of A minor that builds tension before a chorus.
prompts.notes: Should use notes from A natural minor: A B C D E F G

prompts.prompt: Write a simple 4-bar hook with a repeating motif — something catchy at 120 BPM.
prompts.notes: Should use rhythmic repetition (cont or similar)

prompts.prompt: Duplicate the lead melody and shift it up an octave to layer it.
prompts.notes: Adds a second lead channel with n values +12 from original

prompts.prompt: Create a call-and-response pattern between two melodic phrases in the verse.
prompts.notes: Alternating phrases, e.g. 4 beats each

prompts.prompt: Write a pentatonic blues lick in E minor that could serve as a turnaround.
prompts.notes: E minor pentatonic: E G A Bb B D

prompts.prompt: Make the lead melody play the same notes but in triplet rhythm.
prompts.notes: Should modify the 'p' pattern to use triplet durations

prompts.prompt: Add a 2-bar melodic fill at the end of the verse before the chorus hits.
prompts.notes: Should extend or add fill pattern in the verse section

prompts.prompt: Add a ii-V-I chord progression in C major using whole notes.
prompts.notes: Dm7 - G7 - Cmaj7, whole note duration (w)

prompts.prompt: Create a minor 1-4-5 progression in A minor (Am - Dm - Em) in 4/4.
prompts.notes: Each chord 1 bar, notes: Am=57+60+64, Dm=62+65+69, Em=52+55+59

prompts.prompt: Write a four-chord pop progression (I-V-vi-IV) in G major.
prompts.notes: G - D - Em - C, one chord per bar

prompts.prompt: Add lush jazz chords — use 7ths and 9ths — to the chord track.
prompts.notes: Should extend triads to 4+ note voicings

prompts.prompt: Create a descending bass line that outlines a I-VII-VI-V progression in E.
prompts.notes: E D C B bass line, quarter notes

prompts.prompt: Add a suspended 4th chord (sus4) that resolves to the major chord.
prompts.notes: e.g. Csus4 (C F G) resolving to C major (C E G)

prompts.prompt: Write a walking bass line that moves chromatically between chord roots.
prompts.notes: Quarter note bass with chromatic passing tones

prompts.prompt: Create a piano stab pattern — short staccato chords on the off-beats.
prompts.notes: Short note values with rests, syncopated

prompts.prompt: Write a trap hi-hat pattern with 16th-note rolls that speed up mid-bar.
prompts.notes: 16th note (x) hi-hat pattern with velocity variation

prompts.prompt: Create a classic four-on-the-floor kick pattern with a snare on 2 and 4.
prompts.notes: Kick q q q q, snare on beats 2 and 4

prompts.prompt: Add a drum fill at the very end of the verse before the chorus.
prompts.notes: Short 1-bar fill, busy 16th note pattern on snare/kick

prompts.prompt: Write a breakbeat pattern — something chopped and syncopated like a hip-hop break.
prompts.notes: Syncopated kick/snare, 16th notes, varied velocity

prompts.prompt: Create a sparse, minimal kick pattern — just kick on beat 1 and 3.
prompts.notes: p q _q q _q with n for kick

prompts.prompt: Add ghost notes to the snare — very quiet hits between the main snare hits.
prompts.notes: Additional snare hits with low velocity (v ~20-30)

prompts.prompt: Create a bossa nova rhythm — syncopated pattern with kick and rimshot.
prompts.notes: Characteristic bossa nova pattern: kick on 1 and and of 2

prompts.prompt: Write an open hi-hat that hits every other bar for a half-time feel.
prompts.notes: Open hat every 2 bars

prompts.prompt: Extend the chorus from 4 bars to 8 bars.
prompts.notes: arr line: chorus 8 instead of chorus 4

prompts.prompt: Add a 2-bar intro before the verse.
prompts.notes: Adds intro 2 before verse in arr, creates sec intro

prompts.prompt: Add an outro section that's 4 bars long.
prompts.notes: Adds outro 4 at end of arr, creates sec outro

prompts.prompt: Swap the order of the bridge and second chorus.
prompts.notes: Reorders arr line entries

prompts.prompt: Add a pre-chorus section between verse and chorus — 2 bars.
prompts.notes: Adds 'pre 2' between verse and chorus in arr

prompts.prompt: Create a breakdown section where only the bass plays.
prompts.notes: New sec breakdown with only bass ch populated

prompts.prompt: Lower the kick drum volume by 6 dB.
prompts.notes: trackId should be the kick track, volumeDb -6 relative

prompts.prompt: Pan the lead synth 40% to the right.
prompts.notes: pan = 0.4

prompts.prompt: Mute all drum tracks so I can hear only the bass and melody.
prompts.notes: mute=true for all drum tracks

prompts.prompt: Turn up the bass by 3 dB.
prompts.notes: Bass track volumeDb increase by 3

prompts.prompt: Set the BPM to 140.
prompts.notes: bpm=140

prompts.prompt: Slow the tempo down to 95 BPM for a more relaxed feel.
prompts.notes: bpm=95

prompts.prompt: Pan the rhythm guitar hard left and add another panned hard right.
prompts.notes: pan=-1.0 for track, mention second track at pan=1.0

prompts.prompt: Solo the snare so I can hear only it.
prompts.notes: solo=true for snare track

prompts.prompt: Make the synth brighter — open up the filter cutoff.
prompts.notes: Should call get_plugin_params first, then set Cutoff Frequency

prompts.prompt: Add more reverb tail to the pads — increase the decay time.
prompts.notes: ValhallaRoom Decay parameter

prompts.prompt: Make the kick punchier by shortening the decay.
prompts.notes: Kick 3 Main Decay parameter lower value

prompts.prompt: Add automation so the filter sweeps open from bar 1 to bar 4 of the chorus.
prompts.notes: Bird file automation: brightness ramp 0.0 1.0

prompts.prompt: Widen the stereo image of the synth pad.
prompts.notes: Widener Width or Amount parameter

prompts.prompt: Add a sidechain compression effect — duck the pads when the kick hits.
prompts.notes: Ambiguous — sidechaining may not be fully supported, LLM should clarify or note limitation

prompts.prompt: Make it better.
prompts.notes: Ambiguous — LLM must ask what 'better' means before making any edits

prompts.prompt: Change the chords.
prompts.notes: No direction specified — LLM must ask which chords, to what

prompts.prompt: Add some effects.
prompts.notes: No track or effect type specified

prompts.prompt: It sounds off. Fix it.
prompts.notes: Cannot act without knowing what's wrong — must ask

prompts.prompt: Change the lead melody from quarter notes to half notes so it feels more spacious.
prompts.notes: p values should change from q to h (half note implies w split, use w and rest, or two-note groupings); notes stay the same

prompts.prompt: Make the chord pads sustain for 2 bars instead of 1 bar per chord.
prompts.notes: In the arrangement, the section length or note duration should double — chord p values use longer durations

prompts.prompt: Turn the chord hits into staccato 16th notes — really short and punchy.
prompts.notes: p pattern should use x (16th) followed by rest _ for each chord hit

prompts.prompt: Make the bass line alternate between long whole notes and quick 16th note runs.
prompts.notes: Mix of w and x values in the p pattern for bass

prompts.prompt: Convert the melody from 16th notes to dotted 8th notes for a swung feel.
prompts.notes: Dotted feel means alternating longer and shorter durations — e.g. q x pattern

prompts.prompt: Have the snare hit on beat 3 as a whole-bar backbeat, not just a quarter note snap.
prompts.notes: Snare pattern: _ _ q _ (rest for 1+2, quarter snare on 3, rest on 4)

prompts.prompt: Write the chords as an arpeggio — each note of the chord plays one after another as 8th notes ascending.
prompts.notes: Instead of chord clusters in n, write individual notes in sequence with 8th note p values

prompts.prompt: Create a syncopated chord pattern — hit the chord just before beat 1 (anticipation) and hold it through the bar.
prompts.notes: Last 16th of previous bar or use held note pattern; the chord anticipates the downbeat

prompts.prompt: Write a reggae-style chord pattern — stabs on the off-beats (upstrokes on the and of every beat).
prompts.notes: Pattern: _ x _ x _ x _ x (8th rests on beats, 8th chords on off-beats)

prompts.prompt: Create a chord pattern where the root plays on beat 1 and the full chord hits on beat 2.
prompts.notes: Split chord: first n is root note, second n entry is full chord voicing, different p slots

prompts.prompt: Add rhythmic variation to the pad chords — long in bar 1, short stabs in bar 2, creating a 2-bar phrase.
prompts.notes: 2-bar pattern alternating w (whole note) in bar 1 and syncopated x hits in bar 2

prompts.prompt: Write the chords as descending arpeggios — from highest to lowest note in 16th notes.
prompts.notes: Reverse order of notes in the n sequence, x pattern

prompts.prompt: Create a chord pattern that alternates between playing the full chord and a single bass note note each bar.
prompts.notes: Bar 1: full chord with all notes; bar 2: only root note

prompts.prompt: Make the chord track play a Albert bass pattern — alternating root and fifth in quarter notes.
prompts.notes: Root (e.g. C2=36) and fifth (G2=43) alternating: p q q q q, n 36 43 36 43

prompts.prompt: Give the chords a flamenco strum feel — a fast 16th-note strummed pattern from low to high notes.
prompts.notes: Each chord note played sequentially in very fast succession — x x x x pattern ascending

prompts.prompt: Add velocity variation to the hi-hats so they feel less robotic — mix loud and soft hits.
prompts.notes: v values should vary: mix of high (100-110) and low (40-60) velocity across the 16th note hi-hat pattern

prompts.prompt: The snare hits all feel the same volume. Add some subtle velocity variation to make it sound more human.
prompts.notes: Snare v values should vary slightly: e.g. v 90 85 95 80 rather than v 90 90 90 90

prompts.prompt: Add some dynamic variation to the melody — let some notes be louder on the phrase peaks and softer in between.
prompts.notes: v values should follow a contour: louder on melodic peaks, softer on passing tones. Use explicit v line

prompts.prompt: Make the bass line feel more alive by varying the velocity between notes — harder on the downbeats, softer off-beats.
prompts.notes: v pattern: strong on beat 1, weaker on beats 2-4 and off-beats

prompts.prompt: Add subtle pitch variation to the chord pads — use slight chord voicing inversions across bars so it doesn't feel static.
prompts.notes: Could shuffle note order or use +12/-12 offsets on some notes across pattern repetitions

prompts.prompt: This sounds too stiff and quantized. Add some rhythmic looseness — some notes slightly early or late.
prompts.notes: Should use sw (swing) and/or t (timing offsets) to add humanization. E.g. sw 55 ~3 for light swing with jitter, or t with small offsets

prompts.prompt: The music feels repetitive. Add variation in the second verse — change up the melody slightly.
prompts.notes: Second verse (if it exists, or create it) should have melodic variation: different notes or rhythm at the end of phrases

prompts.prompt: Make the chorus feel more energetic — add more notes, higher velocities, and denser rhythms than the verse.
prompts.notes: Chorus sec should have busier patterns (more x notes), higher v values (100-120), and more active parts

prompts.prompt: The arrangement sounds too mechanical. Add a 1-bar variation every 4 bars to break the loop.
prompts.notes: Could mean adding a fill section, or using a 4-bar arrangement unit with slight variation in the last bar

prompts.prompt: Make the verse sparse and the chorus full — strip back the verse to just bass and kick, then bring everything in for the chorus.
prompts.notes: Verse sec: only bass and kick channels populated. Chorus sec: all channels. Tests sparse vs dense arrangement contrast
